_hint: GENERATED by scripts/gen-milb-history.mjs (NOT hand-typed anymore). Re-run that script to refresh. Hand-authored data — per-club/era `note`s, era `logo`s, verified pre-2005 eras, and the top-level `caveats` — lives in scripts/milb-history-seed.json and is merged in each run, so edit the SEED, not this file (edits here are overwritten). See src/api/milbHistory.js for the reader and docs/milb-historical-logos.md for the logo manifest.
method: Eras from 2005 on are derived from statsapi’s own season-scoped snapshot, GET /api/v1/teams?sportId={11|12|13|14}&season={Y}, which reports each club’s name/locationName/league and parentOrgId/parentOrgName AS OF THAT SEASON; consecutive seasons with an equal parent (or name) collapse into one [startYear, endYear] era. This is the ground truth the app’s live lookups (src/api/team.js) miss — those only fetch the CURRENT season and would mislabel a past stint. Pre-2005 eras come from the hand-verified seed (statsapi’s own affiliate data is unreliable before then; see the generator header).
scope: All 30 MLB orgs’ AAA/AA/A+/A affiliates (sportIds 11, 12, 13, 14), seasons 2005–2026 from statsapi plus hand-verified pre-2005 eras from the seed. A club is included only if its parent org OR its own name changed at least once (a club that never changed either is omitted — the live current-season lookup already resolves it correctly). Rookie/complex/DSL levels are out of scope; a (teamId, year) with no matching era below falls through to the live lookup, unchanged — safe by construction.
caveats: statsapi team ids sometimes track a persistent CITY/BRAND slot rather than a literal relocated roster. Example: id 510 ('San Antonio Missions') was Double-A/San Diego Padres through 2018 and became Triple-A/Milwaukee Brewers for 2019-2020 under the SAME id — while the Triple-A club that actually physically relocated INTO San Antonio that year (the Colorado Springs Sky Sox, its own id 551) was left permanently inactive, and the outgoing Double-A Missions' own players/ownership relocated OUT to Amarillo under a brand-new id (5368, first-year-of-play 2018/2019) rather than taking id 510 with them. Press narratives about 'which team physically moved where' do not always match statsapi's id bookkeeping — verify with the season-scoped endpoint above before trusting a Wikipedia 'relocated to' claim at the id level.

Similarly: id 559 (today's Los Angeles Angels affiliate, Rocket City Trash Pandas) was the Milwaukee Brewers' Double-A Huntsville Stars affiliate through 2014, then had NO team attached 2015-2019, before resurfacing in 2020 as the Angels' Rocket City Trash Pandas — so the popular 'Huntsville Stars name returns to the area as the Rocket City Trash Pandas' framing IS correct at the statsapi id level. Most press instead frames the Trash Pandas as a continuation of the Mobile BayBears (a SEPARATE, now-inactive id, 417, whose ownership group did physically relocate the club/PDC slot to Madison, AL in 2020). Both claims are independently true of different real-world entities (an ownership/franchise-agreement lineage vs. a statsapi database id lineage); this file follows the statsapi id, since that is what every stat split in this app is already keyed on.

Year ranges below are the SEASON the club was affiliated with that org, inclusive both ends, per statsapi's own per-season snapshot. A mid-season affiliate switch (rare) would not be captured precisely by this year-granularity model.
Uncertain items are flagged inline with an `uncertain` note; treat those specific boundaries as approximate pending a human double-check.

417.note: statsapi marks this id inactive since the 2019-2020 relocation to Madison, AL (which took a DIFFERENT id, 559 — see caveats). Kept here so a player's pre-2020 Mobile stint still resolves the org he was actually with, not whatever the (now-defunct) id would show as current.

484.note: Only the Brewers window is researched here; this club's other eras (pre-2000, 2005-present under Pittsburgh) are not covered.

parentHistory.note: Triple-A for these two seasons only (the former Colorado Springs Sky Sox slot, id 551, moved in); Double-A in every other season on record, including today.

521.note: The Freddie Freeman example from the original bug report: his 2009 A+ stint here was under the Braves, not the club's current parent (the Cubs, since 2015).

538.note: A pure relocation/rename with NO parent-org change — the Colorado Rockies affiliation carried straight through the 2016 move, so this club has no parentHistory entries, only a nameHistory one.

551.note: statsapi marks this id inactive since the 2019 relocation to San Antonio (which reused a DIFFERENT id, 510 — see caveats).

559.note: See caveats above re: id lineage vs. the Mobile BayBears press narrative.
